title: Agent identity: decouple persona from stack
summary: Implement the Kradle agent identity model by adding AgentPersona, AgentSoul, AgentAppearance, AgentVoiceProfile, and AgentDefinition CRDs, resolving personas independently of AgentStack infrastructure, composing prompts centrally, and preserving legacy AgentStack dispatch.
compatibilityRequirements: AgentStack continues to work with inline systemPrompt, developerPrompt, taskPrompt, and skillRefs as deprecated-but-functional compatibility behavior.
Manual dispatch accepts either agentDefinition or agentStack.
AgentTriggerRule accepts either agentDefinition or agentStack.
AgentDefinition must resolve to its AgentStack before permission review, runtime identity checks, memory snapshot selection, workspace provisioning, context bundle assembly, and job creation.
Prompt composition is centralized and deterministic in soul -> persona -> definition -> stack fallback order.
Do not implement web console persona management from follow-up issue #621 or migration tooling from follow-up issue #622 in this run.
acceptanceCriteria: The five new identity CRDs are defined in resource-model.js and packages/kradle/charts/crds/agent-resources.yaml with matching required fields.
createResource, validateResource, resourceSchemaForKind, storageClassForKind, and kind count tests cover the five new kinds.
Agent persona resolution validates persona references and resolves referenced or inline soul, appearance, and voice profile data.
Prompt composition produces deterministic soul, persona, definition, and legacy stack fallback output with tests proving order.
AgentDefinition dispatch works and preserves the existing permission review and AgentStack runtime path.
Legacy AgentStack dispatch, triggers, MCP stack dispatch, and inline prompt behavior remain functional.
SDK exports include the new persona controller and prompt composition helpers.
MCP exposes kradle_list_agents, kradle_get_agent_profile, and kradle_create_agent with focused tests.
Focused and broad verification commands pass or document only environment-specific residual risk.
